verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is synthesized as a pre-pro-protein. The mature TGF-β1 dimer remains non-covalently associated with its Latency-Associated Peptide (LAP) to form the Small Latent Complex (SLC). The SLC is in turn disulfide-bonded to LTBP (Latent TGF-β Binding Protein) or to transmembrane proteins GARP (LRRC32) or LRRC33, forming a Large Latent Complex (LLC) covalently tethered to the cell surface. On regulatory T cells, platelets, and other immune/stromal cells, the TGFB1 LLC is stably anchored to the outer leaflet via disulfide bond to GARP or LRRC33 — this is a covalent, wash-resistant linkage to a TM scaffold. The dominant soluble pool (secreted/matrix-deposited) would ordinarily push toward secreted_only, but the documented GARP/LRRC33-tethered surface form with covalent anchorage qualifies as stable_surface_attachment. The surface display is restricted to specific cell types and activation states (Tregs, activated platelets, macrophages), making this contextual rather than yes.
